_metadata.task_description: rc.15 Phase 2 command-tree pruning: delete fab hooks (C5), strip config subCommands (C6), delete scan top-level but keep runInitScan (C7), hide plan-context-hint (C8), wire fab config into allCommands (C9)

project_structure: Monorepo (pnpm workspace) with packages/cli, packages/server, packages/shared, packages/dashboard. The CLI is built on citty and exposes commands via packages/cli/src/commands/. Each command file exports both a citty CommandDef (default export) and zero or more pure helpers (e.g., scan.ts exports runInitScan, hooks.ts exports installHooks, config.ts exports installMcpClients + parseClientFilter). The router lives in packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts as a lazy-import allCommands map. install.ts is the meta-orchestrator that programmatically reuses install-side helpers from hooks.ts (installHooks) and config.ts (configCommand.installMcpClients) and scan.ts (runInitScan) — these internal call sites are what makes C5/C6/C7 a surgical command-tree deletion rather than a full code rip.

relevant_files.rationale: The single source of truth for the public command tree. Currently registers install/scan/serve/uninstall/doctor/hooks/plan-context-hint. C5 removes 'hooks', C7 removes 'scan' (but only after scan.ts is restructured), C9 adds 'config'. Every rc.15 command-tree edit touches this file.

key_code.description: Lazy-import dispatch table. 7 entries today; rc.15 target is 5 entries (install, doctor, serve, uninstall, config) — scan + hooks deleted, plan-context-hint either hidden via meta.hidden (preferred) or removed from this map but kept callable through subCommands of a parent command, config added.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: this is the apex node of the command tree. Every C5-C9 mutation either adds/removes an entry here or relies on the entry to remain reachable from the runtime dispatch.

relevant_files.rationale: C5 target: delete entire file's top-level command surface. But the file also defines installHooks() which install.ts:1206 and config.ts:13/105 BOTH import. Cannot delete the whole file in rc.15 — must extract installHooks (and its 9-step orchestration) into a non-command module (e.g., src/install/hooks-orchestrator.ts) before removing the citty wrapper.

key_code.description: Citty defineCommand with name='hooks' and a single subCommand 'install'. Delete this default export and the subCommand wrapper.

9-step idempotent hook+skill installer. KEEP — install.ts:1206 (hooks stage) calls this directly; config.ts re-exports hooksCommand via subCommands but the underlying installHooks call path goes through install.ts. Three integration tests + one unit test exercise installHooks directly.
Internal post-merge validator. Stays with installHooks wherever it moves.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: this file is the prototype 'command-with-shared-helper' problem — deleting the top-level fab hooks must NOT delete the helper that fab install depends on. The split between defineCommand export (delete) and installHooks export (keep) is the canonical pattern for C5/C7.

relevant_files.rationale: C7 target: delete the scanCommand default export but keep runInitScan + createScanReport + __testing__ exports. install.ts:19 imports {detectExistingLanguage, runInitScan, ResolvedLanguage} from './scan.js'; install.ts:926 calls runInitScan post-init. Tests in scan-init.test.ts, scan-edge-cases.test.ts, scan-builders.test.ts import functions directly bypassing the citty wrapper.

key_code.description: Citty defineCommand wrapping runInitScan with --target/--debug/--json flags. DELETE default export; the citty wrapper is the only top-level surface. The folder-rename per task description is 'fold into fab doctor --rescan' so a thin re-implementation should live in doctor.ts.

Async function that reads forensic.json and writes 4-7 baseline knowledge entries. KEEP as named export — install.ts:926 calls this with source:'init', and the future doctor --rescan flag will call it with source:'doctor_fix' (the source enum already includes 'doctor_fix'). Test count: 11 direct callers in scan-init.test.ts alone.

Legacy public API used by scan-edge-cases.test.ts integration test. Header comment L29-L37 explicitly notes this is kept for the test. Decision needed: delete with the legacy test, or migrate the test to runInitScan.
Used by install.ts:19 import. KEEP — orthogonal to the command surface deletion.
Test-only re-exports used by scan-builders.test.ts. KEEP — independent of command wrapper.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: scan.ts demonstrates the inversion of the hooks.ts pattern at a larger scale — 90% of the file is pure helpers consumed by install.ts and tests, only L286-L334 is the citty wrapper. The 'delete top-level command, keep all internals' surgery is mechanical but the surrounding doctor --rescan wiring (in doctor.ts) is the non-trivial part.

relevant_files.rationale: C6 + C9 target. C6: strip subCommands.install and subCommands.hooks from configCmd (L99-L152). C9: register configCmd into allCommands. The default export configCmd is currently DEAD (not wired into index.ts:1-10) — confirmed by reading both files. The installMcpClients helper at L156-L191 stays; install.ts:17 imports {installMcpClients} via 'import * as configCommand'.

key_code.description: Citty defineCommand exposing two subCommands: 'hooks' (re-exports hooksCommand from ./hooks.js — coupled to C5) and 'install' (inline-defined wrapper around installMcpClients). C6: replace subCommands map with a single run() stub printing the rc.16 placeholder message.

Line 13 imports hooksCommand from ./hooks.js; line 105 wires it as the 'hooks' subCommand. Both must be removed for C6 — and because nothing else in config.ts uses hooksCommand, the import can be deleted outright.
Inline citty defineCommand wrapping installMcpClients(). Delete this entire block; installMcpClients itself stays because install.ts:1192 calls it.

Pure async helper that drives per-client MCP writers. KEEP — install.ts:17 imports it as configCommand.installMcpClients, and uninstallMcpClients in uninstall.ts mirrors it. No path through the command tree.
Exported helper. Used by tests (config-install.test.ts:7) only after C6. Decide whether to inline the test against the rc.16 panel or delete the helper.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: config.ts is the most surgically complex file in rc.15 — it's simultaneously (a) the C6 prune target (strip 2 subCommands), (b) the C9 wire target (add to index.ts), and (c) the placeholder for rc.16. The dead-code state (defined but not registered) is the cleanest possible starting position because nothing currently depends on fab config being reachable.

relevant_files.rationale: C8 target: hide from --help. The file stays callable for hook consumers (knowledge-hint-broad.cjs and knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs templates spawn it) but should not appear in 'fab --help' top-level command listing. Citty supports meta.hidden: true (confirmed in node_modules/.pnpm/citty@0.2.2/.../dist/index.d.mts L54: 'hidden?: boolean').

key_code.description: Current meta: {name, description}. Add `hidden: true` to suppress from --help listing. The command remains in allCommands so `fab plan-context-hint` continues to resolve when invoked directly by hook scripts.
Pure handler exported for unit tests. Unaffected by C8.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: C8 is the simplest of the five — pure metadata flag, no callgraph change. The citty 'hidden: true' mechanism is the canonical workaround; the snapshot test in cli-surface.test.ts:94-102 already only asserts the 5-command public set (install, scan, doctor, serve, uninstall), which means plan-context-hint and hooks are already excluded from the contract — the snapshot won't break.

relevant_files.rationale: Critical internal caller of all three command files being modified. L18 imports installHooks, L19 imports runInitScan + detectExistingLanguage, L17 imports * as configCommand (uses configCommand.installMcpClients at L1192). All three imports must keep working after C5/C6/C7. install.ts:926 calls runInitScan post-init; install.ts:1206 calls installHooks in the hooks stage; install.ts:1192 calls installMcpClients in the mcp stage.

key_code.description: Programmatic invocation of runInitScan during stage (b) post-init scan. Wrapped in try/catch with stderr warning on failure. This is the install.ts:740-746 line range referenced in the task description (line numbers are off by ~180 — actual call is at 924-932). Confirms C7's 'keep runInitScan export' is the correct surgery.
Hooks stage: `const result = await installHooks(plan.target, { force: plan.options.force });` — direct function call, no command wrapper. C5 can safely delete hooksCommand without breaking this.
MCP stage: `const result = await configCommand.installMcpClients(plan.target, {...});` — namespace import preserves access after C6 strips the subCommand wrapper.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: install.ts is the proof that the C5/C6/C7 deletions are non-destructive — every removed top-level command has its business logic re-entered from install.ts via direct function calls. The 'top-level command' surface and the 'reusable helper' surface are cleanly separated.

relevant_files.rationale: Drift gate for the public command surface. Line 94-102 explicitly asserts public command set = [install, scan, doctor, serve, uninstall]. After C5+C7 this assertion REMOVES scan, so the test must update to [install, doctor, serve, uninstall, config] — note: config replaces scan. Lines 77-88 also drive per-command snapshot tests; scan's snapshot at lines 198-235 of cli-surface.test.ts.snap becomes orphan and must be deleted.

key_code.description: Asserts exactly 5 commands. For rc.15 this list rotates: -scan, +config; -snapshot import for scanCommand. Note the test currently DOES import scanCommand (L14) — the import + snapshot row + array entry must be removed atomically.

Parametrised snapshot for install/scan/doctor/serve/uninstall. Remove the ['scan', scanCommand] tuple; add ['config', configCmd] tuple (if rc.15 wants a snapshot for the stub config command — recommended to lock the stub message).

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: this test IS the command-tree contract — failing here surfaces any unintended tree change. It must be updated in lock-step with index.ts edits in C5/C7/C9.

relevant_files.rationale: Snapshot file holding the per-command CLI surface. Lines 198-235 are the 'scan' surface snapshot that becomes dead after C7. Lines 78-196 are install (likely needs update too if rc.15 strips install flags per CHANGELOG.md:41). Re-record with `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -u` after edits — drift-hint at L4 documents the procedure.

key_code.description: Snapshot block for fab scan command surface. Delete this entire export after C7.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: snapshot tests are the deterministic mirror of the command tree — any rc.15 PR will produce a snapshot diff here as the visible proof of the contraction.

relevant_files.rationale: Tests both en + zh-CN locale snapshots and imports scanCommand at L68. After C7 removes scanCommand default export, this test must drop the scan locale snapshot (lines 66-71 collect scanOutput). Without the fix, i18n snapshot will fail or the test will not type-check.

key_code.description: Dynamic import of scanCommand and capture of its --help output for the locale snapshot. Remove this block; the scan-on-locale assertion no longer applies once scan is folded into doctor --rescan.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: i18n.test.ts probes the rendered command-surface from a user-locale perspective. Removing a top-level command means removing a locale snapshot row.

relevant_files.rationale: Test for parseClientFilter helper from config.ts. Survives C6 because parseClientFilter is a pure utility that stays exported. The Codex TOML writer test below (line 28+) is orthogonal. NO deletion needed here, but worth checking whether parseClientFilter is still reachable from any production code path after C6 strips the install subCommand. If not, the helper itself may also be deletable — clean-slate per project_v2_rc_continuation policy.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: after C6, parseClientFilter becomes orphaned because no remaining command parses --clients. Decision: keep for rc.16 panel reuse, or delete as dead code now.

relevant_files.rationale: Tests installHooks() directly (L22 import from '../src/commands/hooks.js'). Survives C5 because installHooks stays exported. After C5 the import path changes if installHooks moves to a non-command module (e.g., src/install/hooks-orchestrator.ts) — at minimum, update the import path.

key_code.description: Import path '../src/commands/hooks.js'. If C5 moves installHooks out of /commands/ (recommended — command tree should only host commands), update this import. If installHooks stays in /commands/hooks.ts with the citty wrapper deleted, no change needed.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: this test demonstrates that installHooks is a 'shared helper' not a 'command' — the test bypasses the command surface entirely, validating C5's safety.

relevant_files.rationale: Integration test importing installHooks from ../../src/commands/hooks.ts at L29. Three call sites at L341-L356. Survives C5 same way as hooks-install-validate.test.ts — same import-path update needed if installHooks relocates.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: confirms that two test files exercise installHooks() directly. The C5 surgery must preserve these call paths.

relevant_files.rationale: Heaviest direct caller of runInitScan — 11 invocations (L93, L124, L155, L186, L206, L209, L227, L236, L260, L271, L310, L335, L368). Imports runInitScan from '../../src/commands/scan.ts' at L24. C7 keeps runInitScan as a named export so this test still passes; if scan.ts is renamed or runInitScan relocates, update the import.

key_code.description: The dominant test of runInitScan behaviour (forensic.json required, idempotency, baseline knowledge entries). Must keep working post-C7.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: this test is the strongest evidence that runInitScan is a first-class library function, NOT just a CLI handler. C7's 'keep the function, delete the command' is validated by 11 lines of test calls that never touch the citty wrapper.

relevant_files.rationale: Imports __testing__ from scan.ts (L17). Tests the per-builder template registry. Survives C7 entirely — __testing__ stays exported.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: orthogonal to command surface; only impacted if scan.ts file is renamed.

relevant_files.rationale: Imports createScanReport from scan.ts (L12). The header comment in scan.ts:32 calls out this test as a kept-for-legacy consumer. C7 decision: delete createScanReport + this test together (clean-slate per memory), or keep both as legacy ballast.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: surfaces a clarification need — what gets cleaned up when the top-level scan command dies?

relevant_files.rationale: Mentions runInitScan in a comment at L108 ('runInitScan will preserve meta'). No direct import or call — just documentation. Survives all rc.15 edits.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: confirms runInitScan side-effects are widely understood in tests; renaming the function would require a doc-comment sweep.

relevant_files.rationale: Hook script that spawns `fab plan-context-hint` at runtime. Confirms why C8 hides (not deletes) plan-context-hint — the command must remain callable, just not advertised in --help.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: hidden-not-deleted is the correct outcome for plan-context-hint because runtime consumers exist outside the user-facing surface.

relevant_files.rationale: Lists 'install/scan/doctor/serve' as canonical public commands (L12, L17). C7 + C9 updates needed: remove fab scan line, add fab config (or document fab doctor --rescan as replacement). Also L12: '公共命令面只保留 install/scan/doctor/serve' — drop scan.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: user-facing documentation must mirror index.ts allCommands content; one-line edit but easily forgotten.

relevant_files.rationale: Test seed §1 Feature Surface (L9-L14) is the authoritative spec for the public command set. Drift-gate snapshot in cli-surface.test.ts L21-L24 explicitly references THIS file as the source of truth. C5/C7/C9 require synchronized edits: remove the `scan` bullet (L11), add `config` bullet, mention `doctor --rescan` flag (C7 followup), update §5 source traceability.

key_code.description: Bullet list of 5 public commands. rc.15 swap: remove `scan`, add `config`. Also update §1 'Internal surface' (L17-L23) and §5 (L72-L73).
Mentions deprecated mcp-config command. After C7, add a similar note for the removed fab scan top-level.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: the test seed is the contract that the snapshot test (cli-surface.test.ts) validates against. Edits here gate the snapshot drift.

relevant_files.rationale: L41-L49 already advertises rc.15 plan: kill fab hooks, kill fab config install/hooks, fold fab scan into fab doctor --rescan. Confirms the exploration scope. rc.15 release notes will live above L8 (rc.14 entry).
relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: the changelog is the public-facing record of the contraction. Plan exists; rc.15 PR adds the actual rc.15 entry above L8.

relevant_files.rationale: Citty 0.2.2 type definitions confirm `CommandMeta.hidden?: boolean` (L50-L56). This is the canonical mechanism for C8 — set planContextHintCommand.meta.hidden = true to suppress from --help while keeping the command callable from runtime consumers.

key_code.description: interface CommandMeta { name?, version?, description?, hidden?: boolean, alias? }. The `hidden` field is the citty-native hide-from-help mechanism.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Command-tree-deps angle: resolves the open question from Strategy Step 3 — citty natively supports `meta.hidden`, no workaround needed.

patterns: Pattern 1 — Command + Helper duality: each /commands/*.ts file currently exports both a citty defineCommand (for the CLI surface) and one or more pure async helpers (for cross-command reuse from install.ts). rc.15 deletes the command export but preserves the helpers, validating the pattern as the correct seam for surface contraction. Pattern 2 — Lazy import router: index.ts uses `() => import('./X.js').then(m => m.default)` so each command file's default export is the public registration. Removing an entry from allCommands is the canonical way to delete a top-level command. Pattern 3 — Hidden but callable: citty's `meta.hidden: true` lets a command stay in allCommands (and thus callable) while being absent from --help — used by C8 for plan-context-hint (called by hook scripts at runtime). Pattern 4 — Drift-gate snapshot: cli-surface.test.ts holds an exact 5-element public-command list and per-command argument snapshots. Every rc.15 surface edit MUST update both the assertion at L94-L103 AND re-record snapshots (`pnpm test -u`). Pattern 5 — Stub via rc.16 placeholder: the recommended C9 pattern for fab config is `defineCommand({ meta, run: () => console.log('Config panel coming in rc.16 — edit .fabric/fabric-config.json directly for now'); })` — no args, no subcommands, deterministic output.
dependencies: External: citty@0.2.2 (defineCommand, CommandMeta.hidden, SubCommandsDef). Internal call graph for the five edits: (a) hooks.ts → installHooks consumed by install.ts:1206 and config.ts:13 (the latter goes away in C6); also consumed by 2 test files. (b) scan.ts → runInitScan consumed by install.ts:926; consumed by integration test scan-init.test.ts (11 sites). detectExistingLanguage consumed by install.ts:19. createScanReport consumed only by scan-edge-cases.test.ts. __testing__ consumed by scan-builders.test.ts. (c) config.ts → installMcpClients consumed by install.ts:1192 (via `* as configCommand` namespace import). parseClientFilter consumed only by config-install.test.ts. (d) plan-context-hint.ts → planContext (server import) and resolveDevMode (cli-internal); runPlanContextHint consumed by hook templates knowledge-hint-broad.cjs / knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs via process-spawn (not module import). (e) index.ts → all of the above via lazy dynamic import. No circular dependencies; no implicit string-key registry. The only runtime-string dependency is the hook scripts spawning `fab plan-context-hint` as a subprocess — confirmed by template grep.
integration_points: (1) install.ts is the central integrator — three programmatic calls (installHooks, installMcpClients, runInitScan) keep the install-side functionality intact after surface contraction. (2) doctor.ts is the future home of fab doctor --rescan (C7 follow-up out of this task's scope but adjacent) — should call runInitScan with source:'doctor_fix' (an existing source enum value, see scan.ts:180). (3) cli-surface.test.ts is the drift gate — every command-tree edit must update L94-L103 and re-record snapshots. (4) The README + docs/test-seed/cli.md document-of-truth pair must be edited in lock-step. (5) Hook scripts in templates/hooks/*.cjs subprocess-spawn `fab plan-context-hint` — confirms C8 must hide-not-delete.
constraints: (a) installHooks (in hooks.ts) is shared infrastructure used by install.ts AND tests — cannot delete it when deleting the citty wrapper. (b) runInitScan (in scan.ts) similarly shared with install.ts AND 11+ test sites — must remain a named export. (c) installMcpClients (in config.ts) shared with install.ts via namespace import — survives C6. (d) The cli-surface.test.ts snapshot pins exactly 5 public commands (install/scan/doctor/serve/uninstall); rc.15 rotates this to (install/doctor/serve/uninstall/config) — the snapshot test must be updated in the same PR. (e) Hook scripts spawn `fab plan-context-hint` as a subprocess — hidden-not-deleted is the only safe option for C8. (f) Per memory feedback_clean_slate.md, prefer outright deletion of orphaned helpers (parseClientFilter, createScanReport) over preserving them, UNLESS rc.16 has a concrete reuse plan. (g) Citty 0.2.2's `meta.hidden` field is on CommandMeta (not on subCommand position) — confirmed via type defs.

clarification_needs.question: Where should installHooks (and its 9-step orchestrator + validateHookPaths helper) live after C5 deletes the fab hooks top-level command?
clarification_needs.context: hooks.ts currently mixes the citty defineCommand wrapper (lines 36-68) with the installHooks helper (lines 106-281) plus internal validators. Convention elsewhere in /commands/ keeps a one-command-per-file shape; mixing 'no command, just helpers' breaks the dir's invariant. Two test files (hooks-install-validate.test.ts L22, integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L29) import from '../src/commands/hooks.js' and would need import-path updates if the helper relocates.
options: Move installHooks + validateHookPaths into packages/cli/src/install/hooks-orchestrator.ts (matches existing src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts layout); update 2 test imports + 1 install.ts import
Keep hooks.ts where it is, just delete the defineCommand default export and the InstallHooksArgs type; rename file to packages/cli/src/install/hooks.ts; tests + install.ts imports update once
Leave installHooks in /commands/hooks.ts with no default export (file becomes helper-only despite living in /commands/); minimal import churn but breaks dir convention

clarification_needs.question: Should createScanReport (and its supporting walkFiles / buildRecommendations helpers in scan.ts L63-L1559) be deleted as part of C7, or preserved for the scan-edge-cases.test.ts integration test?
clarification_needs.context: scan.ts:29-37 header comment explicitly notes 'Public legacy API — kept because callers depend on it: scan-edge-cases.test.ts'. The runInitScan/v2 path replaced its functionality. The memory's feedback_clean_slate.md prefers outright removal for pre-user Fabric. The test itself only verifies legacy walk/recommendation behaviour that the v2 init-scan no longer exposes.
options: Delete createScanReport + walkFiles + buildRecommendations + getReadmeQuality + matchesIgnorePattern (legacy block from L63-L1559) AND scan-edge-cases.test.ts in the same C7 PR — true clean-slate
Keep them as dead code with a 'remove in rc.16' TODO marker; minimum churn
Migrate scan-edge-cases.test.ts to assert against runInitScan output instead of createScanReport, then delete the legacy helpers

clarification_needs.question: Should fab config in C9 be wired as a normal entry in allCommands (visible in --help) or registered hidden until rc.16 ships the actual TUI panel?
clarification_needs.context: Phase 2 (rc.15) only delivers the placeholder. Showing `fab config — coming in rc.16` in --help advertises a half-baked surface; hiding it via meta.hidden=true keeps the route reachable while not promising features that don't exist yet. The rc.16 implementation will flip the flag back on.
options: Wire as visible — print rc.16 placeholder message on invocation; matches user expectation 'this command exists'
Wire as hidden (meta.hidden=true) — only reachable via direct typed invocation; flip visible when rc.16 lands
Skip wiring entirely in rc.15 — leave configCmd dead-exported as today; defer both C9 and the placeholder to rc.16

clarification_needs.question: What exact message should fab config print on invocation as the rc.16 placeholder?
clarification_needs.context: Phase 2 spec says 'fab config with no args should print a placeholder pointing to rc.16'. Should match the project's i18n pattern (use t() key) and tone. The CHANGELOG and existing fab install messages are factual + actionable. The task description proposes 'Config panel coming in rc.16 — edit fabric-config.json directly for now'.
options: 'Config panel coming in rc.16. Edit .fabric/fabric-config.json directly until then.' (factual + actionable + correct path)
'fab config is reserved for the rc.16 TUI panel. For now, edit .fabric/fabric-config.json by hand.' (more verbose)
Print rc.16 placeholder via t('cli.config.placeholder') i18n key so en + zh-CN locales render correctly
Print the placeholder AND auto-open .fabric/fabric-config.json in $EDITOR — bridges the gap better

clarification_needs.question: After C6 deletes the fab config install subCommand, should parseClientFilter (config.ts:56-73) be deleted as orphaned dead code, or preserved for rc.16?
clarification_needs.context: parseClientFilter parses comma-separated --clients values into a Set<ClientKind>. Its only production caller was the deleted install subCommand. The only remaining import is config-install.test.ts:7 (unit test on the helper itself). rc.16 may add a `fab config mcp` panel that reuses it. Per project_v2_rc_continuation + feedback_clean_slate, clean-slate removal is preferred when there is no user yet.
options: Delete parseClientFilter + the config-install.test.ts client-aliases describe block in the same PR — clean-slate; rc.16 re-adds if needed
Keep parseClientFilter and its test as future-proof helper; document the orphan status in a comment
Keep parseClientFilter but mark as @internal and add a knip-ignore until rc.16

clarification_needs.question: Should the cli-surface.test.ts public-command-set assertion add 'config' to the 5-command list, or grow to 6 commands (install/doctor/serve/uninstall/config) excluding the deleted scan?
clarification_needs.context: Today the assertion is exactly [install, scan, doctor, serve, uninstall] (5 commands). rc.15 removes scan AND adds config. Net count stays at 5: [install, doctor, serve, uninstall, config]. But if C9 hides fab config (clarification 3 option 2), then the visible set drops to 4 and the assertion must shrink accordingly.
options: Keep the public set at 5: rotate scan out, config in (assuming C9 wires config visible)
Shrink to 4: scan out, config hidden → only install/doctor/serve/uninstall counted
Replace the hard-coded list with a derived assertion (filter allCommands.meta.hidden=false) to auto-track future hide/show flips
